title: Central Bank Communication and Inflation Expectations: High-Frequency Sentiment Extraction
status: Revise & Resubmit, Journal of Monetary Economics
abstract: We construct a novel high-frequency sentiment index from central bank monetary policy announcements and press conferences using transformer-based language models. We find that surprise tone shocks significantly anchor long-term inflation expectations, mitigating market volatility following unexpected policy interest rate decisions.

title: Monetary Policy Shocks and Firm Capital Structure: Evidence from Credit Registers
abstract: Using granular credit register data covering non-financial corporations over two decades, we analyze how monetary policy shocks transmit heterogeneously across firm leverage profiles. High-leverage firms cut employment twice as fast during monetary tightening cycles.

title: Assessing Systemic Liquidity Risks in Non-Bank Financial Intermediaries
abstract: This policy brief reviews recent stress testing frameworks applied to open-ended money market funds and non-bank financial intermediaries (NBFIs), highlighting policy recommendations for macroprudential oversight.
